votes.reason: The Python code faithfully translates the Coq logic, including the string splitting, whitespace trimming, and prefix checking, ensuring identical behavior on all concrete inputs.

The Python code faithfully implements the logic of the Coq definitions, including the custom string splitting behavior, whitespace trimming, and the specific boredom check (checking if the sentence starts with 'I').
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the string splitting, whitespace trimming, and prefix checking logic, ensuring identical behavior on all concrete inputs.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. Both split strings by sentence delimiters ('.', '?', '!'), trim leading spaces, check if sentences start with 'I', and count them. The split_aux function correctly accumulates characters and appends on delimiters, matching the Coq recursive behavior. trim_leading_whitespace only considers space characters in both versions. The prefix check uses startswith which matches the Coq prefix function. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

The logic matches the Coq implementation for all concrete string inputs.
